tex:
**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 1]**

Rectangle A measures 3 cm by 8 cm.

Five rectangles congruent to A are joined to make a shape.

Work out the perimeter of this shape.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 2]**

Find the highest odd number that is a factor of 60 and a factor of 90.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 3]**

Mrs Salaman gives her class two mathematics tests.
The scatter diagram shows information about the marks each student scored.

(a) Write down the highest mark scored on test 1.

(b) Write down the type of correlation shown in the scatter diagram.

(c) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.

(d) Hamish scored a mark of 40 on test 1.
He was absent for test 2.
Use your line of best fit to find an estimate for his mark on test 2.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 4]**

A bag contains blue, red, yellow and green balls only.
A ball is taken from the bag at random.
The table shows some information about the probabilities.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Colour & Probability \\
\hline
Blue & 0.15 \\
\hline
Red & 0.2 \\
\hline
Yellow & \\
\hline
Green & 0.43 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.

(b) Abdul takes a ball at random and replaces it in the bag.
He does this 200 times.
Find how many times he expects to take a red ball.
texHtml:
**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 4]** A bag contains blue, red, yellow and green balls only. A ball is taken from the bag at random. The table shows some information about the probabilities.
ColourProbability
Blue0.15
Red0.2
Yellow
Green0.43
(a) Complete the table. (b) Abdul takes a ball at random and replaces it in the bag. He does this 200 times. Find how many times he expects to take a red ball.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 5]**

(a) The nth term of a sequence is $60 - 8n$.
Find the largest number in this sequence.

(b) Here are the first five terms of a different sequence.
12, 19, 26, 33, 40

Find an expression for the nth term of this sequence.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 7]**

$234 = 2^3 \times 3^2 \times 13$

$1872 = 2^4 \times 3^2 \times 13$

$234 \times 1872 = 438\ 048$

Use this information to write 438 048 as a product of its prime factors.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 8]**

Without using a calculator, work out

\[\frac{6}{7} \times 2 - \frac{3}{8} \div \frac{25}{1}\]

You must show all your working and give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 9]**

Factorise completely.

(a) $21a^2 + 28ab$

(b) $20x^2 - 45y^2$

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 12]**

Gemma records the times, in seconds, taken for a group of children and a group of adults to complete a puzzle.
The box-and-whisker plot shows information about the times taken for the children to complete the puzzle.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Find the interquartile range of the times taken for the children to complete the puzzle.

(b) The table shows some information about the times, in seconds, taken for the adults to complete the puzzle.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
 & Adults \\
\hline
Minimum & 28 \\
\hline
Lower quartile & 42 \\
\hline
Median & 58 \\
\hline
Upper quartile & 70 \\
\hline
Maximum & 75 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

On the grid above, draw the box-and-whisker plot for the adults.
texHtml:

**[2020 May/June, 0580/21, Question 18]**

P = 2(w + h)
w = 12 correct to the nearest whole number.
h = 4 correct to the nearest whole number.
Work out the upper bound for the value of P.
